user_story: As a developer, I can run AgenC with no prompt in a TTY and get the interactive terminal UI.
expected_behavior: When stdin and stdout are TTYs and no routing flag forces headless mode, the CLI boots the Ink TUI with an empty composer.
test_result: PASS: npm run build succeeded, then check:tui-runtime-startup imported dist/tui/main.js and cleanly spawned agenc in PTYs at 148x40, 120x30, and 80x24.

user_story: As a developer, I can pass a prompt on the CLI and have it preloaded/submitted through the TUI path.
expected_behavior: In an interactive TTY with argv prompt text, routing strips startup flags and boots the TUI with initialPrompt and startup image metadata.
test_result: PASS: agenc.cli-branch contract verified TTY argv routes to bootTUI with initialPrompt; agenc.test verified bootTUI streams startup prompt/images as one daemon message.

user_story: As a script author, I can run one-shot headless prompts and receive terminal output without the TUI.
expected_behavior: -p or --print routes before TTY branches to the daemon-backed one-shot path with startup images preserved.
test_result: PASS: agenc.cli-branch and agenc.test contract coverage verified oneShotCLI routing/daemon prompt-agent behavior for non-TUI startup paths, including startup image forwarding.

user_story: As a script author, I can force one-shot mode even from an interactive terminal.
expected_behavior: --no-tui routes to oneShotCLI after resume/continue/print handling and strips the flag from prompt text.
test_result: PASS: agenc.cli-branch verified --no-tui forces oneShotCLI in an interactive TTY and forwards startup images to oneShotCLI.

user_story: As a shell user, I can pipe text into AgenC and avoid TUI escape output.
expected_behavior: Non-TTY stdin routes to oneShotCLI; stdout non-TTY fallback also avoids the TUI.
test_result: PASS: agenc.cli-branch verified piped stdin plus argv routes to oneShotCLI and not bootTUI; project-trust-preflight covers piped one-shot trust preflight.

user_story: As a returning user, I can resume a specific prior session in an interactive terminal.
expected_behavior: --resume or -r with a non-empty id routes to resumeTUI; non-TTY resume returns exit 2 with a clear error.
test_result: PASS: agenc.cli-branch verified --resume and --resume=<id> dispatch through resumeTUI and non-TTY resume classifies as error; resume-session tests covered latest/session id resolution.

user_story: As a returning user, I can continue the latest project session.
expected_behavior: --continue or -c routes to continueTUI separately from plain agenc, which starts fresh.
test_result: PASS: agenc.cli-branch verified --continue dispatches through continueTUI; resume-session tests verified newest project session resolution for --continue.

user_story: As a user, I can pick provider/model for this session without editing config.
expected_behavior: --provider and --model are stripped from prompt text, read during startup selection, and applied to session configuration.
test_result: PASS: bin-route verified consumed --model/--provider flags are stripped from prompt text; startup-selection tests verified provider/model resolution precedence; model-options-xai tests passed in targeted startup batch.

user_story: As a user with named config profiles, I can select a profile for one run.
expected_behavior: --profile is stripped from prompt text and read by startup selection/config resolution.
test_strategy: targeted startup-selection unit tests;manual config profile smoke
test_result: PASS: startup-selection and config profile coverage passed in the 37-file CLI batch with 581 tests, verifying --profile startup selection and profile-aware config resolution.

user_story: As a user, I can choose the startup approval behavior from the CLI.
expected_behavior: --permission-mode accepts user-addressable modes; yolo/bypass flags map to bypass permissions behavior; unsafe skip flag bypasses approval prompts.
test_result: PASS: permission-mode tests verified external/internal modes and cycling; check:tui-runtime-startup cleanly spawned agenc --yolo at three PTY sizes.

user_story: As a user, I can attach images to the startup prompt.
expected_behavior: Repeated --image values are preserved through routing and normalized into prompt content parts.
test_result: PASS: agenc.cli-branch verified repeated --image values are forwarded to bootTUI/oneShotCLI; agenc.test verified startup prompt and images stream as one daemon message; attachment integration tests passed.
